data.author: William White

data.title: "Tariff Math" Hits Coffee Consumers, Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Shrugs it Off
data.description:
Coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) has long had its detractors, pointing out the high-priced nature of Starbucks products. And while many shrugged these off, as evidenced by Starbucks' quarterly sales figures, the idea that, perhaps, a less-expensive treat could be had elsewhere was hard to buck. Reports note that "tariff math" is starting to hit coffee buyers, and impact sales. But Starbucks investors brushed this off, and Starbucks shares gained modestly in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Coffee prices have been on the rise for the last several years, and coffee buffs are not taking the tide of price hikes lightly. Many are cutting out Starbucks runs in response, switching to home-brewed coffee instead. A Consumer Price Index study revealed that coffee prices were up 18.3% in January, when measured against January 2025. The numbers are even worse going back five years; the difference between January 2026 and January 2021, reports note, is 47%. Increased tariffs have prompted customers to perform "tariff math" to discover how deep the problem goes.

While coffee consumption overall is holding stable, there are signs that the market is starting to fracture, splitting away from higher-end beverages in favor of simpler choices. Sharon Cooksie from Greensboro, North Carolina, perhaps said it best: "I can buy a bag of coffee for $6? It was like I had just discovered another world. The multiverse opened up to me in the coffee aisle of Publix."

The Boba Bubble

One of those alternatives for some is boba tea, and indeed, Starbucks had a hand in getting one bubble tea chain, Gong Cha, off the ground. Gong Cha's founder, Martin Berry, noted that in the early days, he would open Gong Cha locations next to Starbucks outlets where he could. This gave him access to some of Starbucks' spillover business, and let the store take off.

Given that Gong Cha now has around 2,200 stores in 33 countries, the plan must have worked, and worked quite well. It represents another problem for Starbucks, though, in that it is another competitor in a market that is seeing stagnant growth by some reports. We have already seen a range of competitors step in to get a piece of Starbucks' market, but that market is not growing much, if at all.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 15 Buys, eight Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 17.02% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $100.59 per share implies 6.07% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock (LLY) Rises on Plan to Invest $1 Billion in India
data.description:
The stock of Eli Lilly (LLY) is up about 1% after the pharmaceutical company announced plans to invest $1 billion in India as part of its global expansion.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, Eli Lilly is allocating $1 billion to bolster its contract manufacturing operations across India. The company said in a news release that it plans to leverage India as a manufacturing hub to serve international markets, particularly in Asia.

Beyond growing its manufacturing capabilities, Eli Lilly also intends to introduce additional products to the Indian market, including its bestselling Alzheimer's medication Donanemab. India is rapidly becoming a major center for prescription drug production due to its access to Asia and the Middle East.

Eli Lilly's Investments

Eli Lilly has been ramping up its global investments as it markets and sells its blockbuster weight-loss drug Zepbound around the world. Since 2020, Eli Lilly has committed over $50 billion to expanding its U.S. manufacturing footprint, driven by surging demand for its obesity medication.

Previously, Eli Lilly announced a $1 billion investment to expand its manufacturing capabilities in Hyderabad, India. With the company planning to launch a pill version of its weight-loss medication this spring, Eli Lilly's manufacturing investments are expected to increase throughout 2026.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Software Stocks Are Plummeting… Are Their Forward Estimates Next?
data.description:
Artificial intelligence is shaking up the software industry. Indeed, lightning-fast advances from companies like OpenAI, Google (GOOGL), and Anthropic are leading to serious worries about how traditional software businesses will compete. As a result, former high-fliers such as Workday (WDAY) and Salesforce (CRM) are seeing sharp declines in their stock prices. Even so, many Wall Street analysts have been slow to adjust their earnings forecasts, which suggests that they may not fully appreciate the long-term disruption risk.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That gap becomes clear when looking at the numbers. According to Goldman Sachs (GS) strategist Ben Snider, software stocks tracked by the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) have fallen about 24% over the past three months. However, during that same period, two-year forward earnings estimates for the group have actually increased by 5%. Likewise, other industries seen as vulnerable to AI, including insurance brokers and advertising companies, have also received positive revisions to their 2026 earnings estimates.

As a result, some strategists believe that the gap will eventually close, and not in a good way. This is because historically, price action often reflects underlying risks before analysts adjust their forecasts. Software stocks that once traded at price-to-earnings multiples near 35 times are now below 20 times, according to Innovator Capital Management's Tim Urbanowicz, who spoke to Yahoo Finance. He also went on to say that this sell-off highlights the "dark side" of AI and serves as a warning that disruption could spread to other sectors as well.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IGV stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $128.86 per share implies 59.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: LMT, NOC, RTX: U.S. Contractors Eye Canada's New $6.6 Billion Defense Strategy
data.description:
Canada's government has announced plans to spend $6.6 billion on military equipment as it looks to bolster its defense, news that is attracting interest from U.S. contractors.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said that the new defense spending is aimed at supporting Canadian businesses and creating jobs, as well as position Canada away from an overreliance on the neighboring U.S. for military support.

Carney's government aims to increase Canada's defense exports by 50% and industry revenues related to defense by 240%. However, Canada's domestic defense industry is very small and the country has always relied on U.S. contractors such as Lockheed Martin (LMT), Northrop Grumman (NOC) and RTX Corp. (RTX) for its fighter jets, naval warships and tanks.

Driving Job Creation in Canada

Prime Minister Carney has said that his government wants to use the $6.6 billion in defense spending to create 125,000 new Canadian defense jobs over the next 10 years. Achieving those jobs numbers will likely require Canada to bring in U.S. defense contractors.

In the past, Canada's military procurement process has seen large contracts awarded to U.S. companies such as Lockheed Martin on condition that they spend a portion of the money within Canada and create a set number of Canadian jobs. Analysts say they expect a similar approach this time.

Is LMT Stock a Buy?

Lockheed Martin's stock has a consensus Hold rating among 16 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy, 11 Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LMT price target of $636.73 implies 2.72% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Stock (NCLH) Rallies after Activist Elliott Builds 10% Stake
data.description:
Shares of Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) are rallying nearly 11% in regular trading after activist investor Elliott Investment Management disclosed a more than 10% stake in the struggling cruise operator. According to the Wall Street Journal, the activist investor is looking for a full turnaround, including a proxy fight. Elliott cited Norwegian's constant underperformance versus rivals Royal Caribbean (RCL) and Carnival (CCL), who have rebounded strongly after the COVID-19 slump.

Norwegian is the fourth-largest cruise operator globally, with premium brands under its umbrella. NCLH stock has lost 3.7% year-to-date and has trailed the S&P 500 (SPX) index with a 21% decline over the past year. Despite recovering consumer demand, shares trade near pandemic-era levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Figma Stock (FIG) Rises after Launching a New Anthropic Integration
data.description:
Creative design platform Figma (FIG) is taking steps to address investor concerns that artificial intelligence could disrupt its business. To do so, the company has partnered with AI developer Anthropic to launch a new integration called "Claude Code to Figma." Interestingly, the feature allows users to bring Claude Code workflows directly into Figma, where the generated code can automatically be converted into editable design frames.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In other words, instead of switching between coding tools and design platforms, users can move their work seamlessly onto the Figma canvas. According to Gui Seiz, Figma's director of AI design, each captured screen can be copied and pasted into any Figma file, thereby instantly turning it into a workable frame. From there, designers can organize, duplicate, refine, and share the layout as they would with any other design element.

More importantly, teams can add notes on what works, highlight unclear areas, and test alternative ideas without having to jump back into the code or make large structural edits. Users can also capture multiple screens in a single session, which helps speed up the design process. For Anthropic, the partnership adds to its growing list of software collaborations as it continues to become a dominant force in the space. This has allowed it to recently close a $30 billion Series G funding round that valued the company at approximately $380 billion.

Is FIG Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on FIG stock based on three Buys, four Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average FIG price target of $42.17 per share implies 86.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today and What Goldman Sachs Predicts Next
data.description:
Markets are edging lower today, reflecting continued caution around technology stocks as investors return from the long Presidents Day weekend. The S&P 500 is down about 0.5%, while the Nasdaq is off ~0.7%, with much of the pressure concentrated in software and AI names.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

After a few days away from their screens, investors are coming back to the same lingering question that has weighed on sentiment for weeks: Is AI creating more opportunity – or more disruption – than the market has priced in?

AI disruption worries have spread into software providers, wealth management platforms, logistics companies, and even parts of real estate. Investors are wondering whether AI tools can replicate or automate core functions, and what that means for traditional providers. Those fears have already contributed to multiple losing weeks for the S&P 500, and today's move suggests that anxiety remains elevated.

The pullback comes despite relatively encouraging macro data. Last week's consumer price index reading came in softer than expected, and the prior jobs report surprised to the upside. Under normal circumstances, cooling inflation and steady employment growth would support equities. Yet, the AI disruption fears are overshadowing those positives – at least for now.

So what does Goldman Sachs expect from here?

In a recent macro update, Goldman Sachs Chief Economist Jan Hatzius maintained what he describes as an "optimistic baseline outlook for 2026," arguing that global growth "remains largely unchanged." Hatzius expects global activity to outperform consensus forecasts, helped by fading tariff effects, fiscal support, and easier financial conditions. In other words, while markets may be wrestling with sector-level uncertainty, Goldman does not see the broader economy rolling over.

Hatzius also notes that inflation is continuing to move toward target in most major economies. The strategist expects further moderation in wages and rents, which should support risk assets — although he cautions that "high valuations limit expected returns" and that uncertainty around AI winners and losers could keep markets choppy in the absence of major shocks.

On the U.S. front, Hatzius sees some mixed signals but not a recession. Recent consumer data has softened, and labor market risks remain tilted to the downside. Still, the strategist places the probability of recession over the next 12 months at a moderate 20%. Moreover, he expects the Federal Reserve to remain on hold for now, while still penciling in potential rate cuts later in the year as inflation trends lower.

Is the S&P 500 a Buy?

Based on TipRanks' ETF Technical Analysis, the technical picture for the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) currently leans bearish. The 1-day summary is flashing a Sell signal, with 13 indicators pointing bearish versus just 5 bullish readings. Both the Oscillators and Moving Averages components are also registering Sell signals, reflecting near-term downside momentum. While the MACD indicator is modestly constructive, the ETF is trading below its 20-day and 50-day exponential moving averages, reinforcing the cautious tone.
data.author: Ben Yoffe
